name: Spend increased without proportional conversion growth
condition_description: Cost increased, but conversions did not grow proportionally.
why_it_matters: Budget is scaling faster than results, which can reduce efficiency and hide wasted spend.
recommendation: Review campaign-level spend allocation, bidding strategy, search terms, asset groups, and audience/geographic segments.

name: ROAS dropped significantly
condition_description: ROAS decreased materially compared with the previous period.
why_it_matters: Revenue efficiency is declining, which can reduce contribution margin and make scaling unsafe.
recommendation: Identify campaigns, products, audiences, geographies, and queries responsible for the ROAS decline.

name: Spend with zero conversions
condition_description: Campaign spent a meaningful amount during the latest period but generated zero conversions.
why_it_matters: Budget may be going to low-intent traffic, weak placements, poor queries, or there may be a tracking issue.
recommendation: Review traffic quality, search terms, placements, landing pages, conversion tracking, and exclusions.

name: CPA increased significantly
condition_description: Cost per conversion increased materially compared with the previous period.
why_it_matters: Acquisition efficiency is deteriorating and can reduce profitability even if conversion volume is stable.
recommendation: Check bidding changes, CPC increases, query quality, audience shifts, landing page conversion rate, and budget allocation.

name: Conversion value missing or flat
condition_description: Campaign has conversions but conversion value is zero or materially missing.
why_it_matters: Smart bidding may optimize against incomplete value signals, making ROAS-based decisions unreliable.
recommendation: Verify primary conversion actions, purchase value tracking, enhanced conversions, and imported revenue values.

name: Active campaign with no impressions
condition_description: Campaign is enabled but received no impressions in the latest period.
why_it_matters: The campaign may be blocked by budget, targeting, policy, bidding, or setup issues.
recommendation: Check status, budgets, bid strategy, targeting, ad approvals, assets, and policy restrictions.

name: Spend concentration risk
condition_description: One campaign receives a disproportionately high share of total account spend.
why_it_matters: Overconcentration can hide performance issues and increase dependency on a single campaign or algorithmic segment.
recommendation: Review spend allocation across campaigns and check whether the dominant campaign is actually the most profitable one.

name: Conversions dropped while spend did not decrease
condition_description: Conversions declined while spend stayed stable or increased.
why_it_matters: This indicates deteriorating traffic quality, auction efficiency, conversion rate, or tracking reliability.
recommendation: Review search terms, CPC, CTR, landing page conversion rate, device/geographic segments, and recent account changes.

name: Enabled campaign has no impressions
condition_description: Campaign is enabled but received no impressions in the latest period.
why_it_matters: The campaign may be blocked by targeting, budget, bidding, policy, ad assets, or setup issues.
recommendation: Review campaign status, ad approvals, budget, bid strategy, geo targeting, audiences, assets, and policy limitations.

name: Search campaign includes Display Network
condition_description: Search campaign appears to have Display Network/content network enabled.
why_it_matters: Display expansion can mix intent levels and make Search performance harder to evaluate.
recommendation: Review network settings and separate Search and Display traffic unless there is a clear strategic reason.

name: Search Partners enabled
condition_description: Search Partners are enabled for the campaign.
why_it_matters: Search Partners can perform differently from Google Search and may dilute performance if not monitored separately.
recommendation: Review Search Partners performance and disable if it is inefficient or not aligned with campaign goals.

name: Maximize Conversions without value guardrail
condition_description: Campaign uses Maximize Conversions while conversion value is important or ROAS is being evaluated.
why_it_matters: If the business goal is revenue or margin, volume-based bidding may optimize for more conversions rather than better-value conversions.
recommendation: Review whether Maximize Conversion Value, target ROAS, or a value-based strategy is more appropriate.

name: Conversions present but conversion value missing
condition_description: Campaign generated conversions but conversion value is zero in the latest period.
why_it_matters: Value-based optimization and ROAS analysis become unreliable when revenue/value signals are missing.
recommendation: Verify purchase value tracking, primary conversion actions, enhanced conversions, imported values, and attribution setup.

name: Performance Max campaign requires deeper asset group audit
condition_description: Performance Max campaign is active and should be audited at asset group, listing group, audience signal, and search category level.
why_it_matters: PMax can hide inefficient segments unless asset groups, product groups, search categories, audience signals, and creative assets are reviewed separately.
recommendation: Add a dedicated PMax audit module for asset groups, listing groups, audience signals, creatives, and search category insights.

name: Campaign naming does not clearly indicate brand/non-brand segmentation
condition_description: Campaign name does not clearly indicate whether traffic is brand, non-brand, competitor, shopping, PMax, remarketing, or prospecting.
why_it_matters: Weak naming conventions make account analysis, reporting, and budget governance harder.
recommendation: Review naming conventions and ensure campaign names clearly reflect campaign type, funnel stage, market, and intent segment.

name: No primary conversion actions detected
condition_description: No active primary conversion actions were detected in the account.
why_it_matters: Google Ads bidding and reporting may not have a reliable primary optimization signal.
recommendation: Verify that the correct lead, purchase, or revenue-driving conversion actions are set as primary.

name: Multiple primary conversion actions may dilute optimization
condition_description: Multiple primary conversion actions are active and included in the conversions metric.
why_it_matters: Smart bidding may optimize toward mixed actions with different business value.
recommendation: Review whether all primary conversions represent meaningful business outcomes and move softer actions to secondary if needed.

name: Purchase or lead conversion action missing
condition_description: No clear purchase or lead conversion action was detected.
why_it_matters: The account may be optimizing toward proxy events rather than actual business outcomes.
recommendation: Confirm that purchase, lead, booking, form submission, or other revenue-driving actions are implemented and set correctly.

name: Conversion value may be missing
condition_description: Primary conversion actions appear to use zero or missing default value.
why_it_matters: ROAS and value-based bidding become unreliable when conversion value is missing or incorrectly configured.
recommendation: Verify dynamic purchase values, imported revenue, enhanced conversions, and value settings for primary conversion actions.

name: Always use default value enabled
condition_description: A conversion action is configured to always use the default value.
why_it_matters: Static values can hide order value differences and reduce the quality of value-based optimization.
recommendation: Use dynamic transaction values where possible, especially for ecommerce and variable lead value models.

name: Inactive or removed conversion actions present
condition_description: Inactive or removed conversion actions are still present in the account.
why_it_matters: Legacy conversion actions can confuse reporting and make audit interpretation harder.
recommendation: Review old conversion actions and clean up or document legacy tracking setup.

name: Legacy attribution model detected
condition_description: A conversion action appears to use an older attribution model instead of a modern/default data-driven model.
why_it_matters: Attribution model differences can distort campaign performance comparisons and optimization signals.
recommendation: Review attribution settings and align them with current Google Ads best practices and reporting requirements.

name: Search term spent without conversions
condition_description: Search term spent a meaningful amount but generated zero conversions.
why_it_matters: Budget may be wasted on low-intent or irrelevant queries.
recommendation: Review the query and add it as an exact or phrase negative if it is not relevant.

name: High-click query with no conversions
condition_description: Search term generated a meaningful number of clicks but no conversions.
why_it_matters: The query may be attracting traffic that does not match the offer, landing page, or conversion intent.
recommendation: Review search intent, landing page relevance, match type, and negative keyword opportunities.

name: Low CTR search term
condition_description: Search term has impressions but weak click-through rate.
why_it_matters: Low CTR may indicate weak relevance, poor ad copy alignment, or broad-match leakage.
recommendation: Review ad relevance, keyword mapping, match type, and whether the term should be excluded.

name: Informational intent query detected
condition_description: Search term appears to have informational intent rather than commercial intent.
why_it_matters: Informational traffic can consume budget without strong purchase or lead intent.
recommendation: Review whether informational queries should be excluded, separated into a different campaign, or handled through SEO/content.

name: Competitor query detected
condition_description: Search term appears to reference a competitor or comparison intent.
why_it_matters: Competitor traffic can be expensive and may convert differently from direct-intent queries.
recommendation: Review competitor query performance separately and decide whether to isolate, reduce bids, or exclude.

name: Brand query appearing in non-brand campaign
condition_description: Brand-like query appears in a campaign that does not seem to be a brand campaign.
why_it_matters: Brand traffic mixed into non-brand campaigns can inflate performance and distort scaling decisions.
recommendation: Separate brand and non-brand traffic and add brand negatives to non-brand campaigns where appropriate.

name: Non-brand query appearing in brand campaign
condition_description: Non-brand query appears in a campaign that seems to be intended for brand traffic.
why_it_matters: Non-brand traffic in brand campaigns can distort brand reporting and budget control.
recommendation: Review campaign query mapping and add negatives or split campaigns by intent.

name: Query has poor ROAS
condition_description: Search term generated spend and conversion value but ROAS is materially below the acceptable threshold.
why_it_matters: Some converting queries may still be unprofitable if value is too low relative to cost.
recommendation: Review query profitability, bid strategy, match type, landing page, and potential exclusions.

name: PMax campaign requires asset group review
condition_description: Performance Max campaign is active and should be reviewed beyond campaign-level metrics.
why_it_matters: PMax campaign-level metrics can hide inefficient asset groups, product groups, audiences, and creative assets.
recommendation: Review asset groups, listing groups, audience signals, product/category splits, search categories, and creative asset coverage.

name: PMax asset group spent without conversions
condition_description: Asset group spent a meaningful amount but generated no conversions.
why_it_matters: Budget may be concentrated in asset groups that are not producing business outcomes.
recommendation: Review asset group theme, audience signal, landing page, product/category mapping, and creative relevance.

name: PMax asset group has low ROAS
condition_description: Asset group generated spend and conversion value but ROAS is materially below the acceptable threshold.
why_it_matters: Some asset groups may convert but still be unprofitable.
recommendation: Review product/category mix, asset quality, landing page relevance, and whether the asset group should be restructured.

name: PMax spend concentration in one asset group
condition_description: One asset group receives a disproportionately high share of PMax campaign spend.
why_it_matters: Overconcentration can reduce learning diversity and hide inefficient segmentation.
recommendation: Review whether spend concentration matches business priority and actual profitability.

name: PMax asset group active but no impressions
condition_description: Asset group is enabled but received no impressions.
why_it_matters: The asset group may be blocked by setup, policy, targeting, assets, or campaign learning behavior.
recommendation: Review asset group status, assets, landing page, policy approvals, product eligibility, and campaign budget.

name: PMax campaign has only one asset group
condition_description: PMax campaign appears to have only one asset group.
why_it_matters: A single asset group can limit segmentation and make it harder to control product/category/audience intent.
recommendation: Consider splitting asset groups by product category, service line, funnel intent, margin group, or audience theme.

name: PMax asset group naming is unclear
condition_description: Asset group name does not clearly indicate product category, service, audience, market, or intent.
why_it_matters: Weak asset group naming makes optimization, reporting, and creative/product mapping harder.
recommendation: Improve naming conventions so each asset group clearly reflects its purpose.

name: Geographic segment spent without conversions
condition_description: A geographic segment spent a meaningful amount but generated zero conversions.
why_it_matters: Budget may be leaking into locations that do not produce business outcomes.
recommendation: Review location performance and consider exclusions, bid adjustments, or campaign splits.

name: Geographic segment has poor ROAS
condition_description: A geographic segment generated spend and value but ROAS is materially below target.
why_it_matters: Underperforming locations can reduce account-level profitability.
recommendation: Review location bid strategy, targeting settings, budget allocation, and location exclusions.

name: Device segment spent without conversions
condition_description: A device segment spent a meaningful amount but generated zero conversions.
why_it_matters: Device-level performance differences can hide wasted spend and conversion rate problems.
recommendation: Review device performance, landing page UX, checkout/form behavior, and bid strategy.

name: Device segment has poor ROAS
condition_description: A device segment generated spend and value but ROAS is materially below target.
why_it_matters: One device type may be dragging down overall campaign efficiency.
recommendation: Review device-level ROAS, conversion rate, CPC, and user experience.

name: Day of week spent without conversions
condition_description: A day-of-week segment spent a meaningful amount but generated zero conversions.
why_it_matters: Budget may be spent on days that do not produce results.
recommendation: Review day-of-week performance and consider ad schedule adjustments if the pattern is consistent.

name: Hour of day spent without conversions
condition_description: An hour-of-day segment spent a meaningful amount but generated zero conversions.
why_it_matters: Budget may be leaking during low-intent or low-conversion hours.
recommendation: Review hourly performance and consider schedule adjustments if the pattern is consistent.

name: Spend concentrated in one segment
condition_description: One geo, device, day, or hour segment receives a disproportionate share of spend.
why_it_matters: Spend concentration can hide risk and reduce control over optimization.
recommendation: Validate that spend concentration matches actual profitability and business priorities.
